serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Golden Pineapple Water is one glass, which is approximately 300 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from input data, which cited HPB Foodo. Values are consistent with sweetened fruit juice.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load for 100+ Foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Estimated based on 'Pineapple juice, canned, unsweetened' (GI 46) and 'Soft drink, regular, carbonated' (GI 63). Given the high sugar content (11.67g/100g) and low fiber, a medium-to-high GI is appropriate. A value of 66 is used, reflecting a sweetened fruit beverage.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (11.7g/100g) in liquid form
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage due to its high sugar content, which independently raises blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (11.7g/100g) and low fiber (0.5g/100g) leading to rapid glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: This sugar-sweetened beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it can cause rapid and significant spikes in blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High intake of added sugars is linked to increased risk of cardiovascular disease
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: To reduce cardiovascular risk, individuals should limit consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages like Golden Pineapple Water due to its high sugar content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Pineapple Water, known as 黄金黄梨水 (Huang Jin Huang Li Shui) in Chinese, is a refreshing pineapple-based beverage popular in Chinese cuisine, particularly as an accompaniment to hot pot. It is typically prepared by blending or juicing pineapples, mixing the juice with water, and sweetening it before chilling. The drink has a sweet and tangy taste, characteristic of pineapple, and a smooth, liquid texture. Nutritionally, it is high in sugar (11.67g/100g) and carbohydrates (12.67g/100g), with low protein and fat. While it contains some Vitamin C from the pineapple, its high sugar content makes it a concern for conditions like Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Cardiovascular Disease, as liquid sugars are rapidly absorbed and can contribute to adverse health outcomes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is from a reliable source (HPB Singapore via GrabFood), but GI is an analogous estimate due to the specific sweetened beverage formulation.
notes: Potassium (80mg/100g) and water (not available) were estimated based on typical pineapple juice values. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed data for these specific nutrients in this sweetened beverage formulation were not available from approved sources.
